decisions.context: SaveRoom needed a clear brand identity that differentiates from scalpers and resellers.
options_considered: Compete on price alone (race to the bottom)
Position as premium/curated (higher margins, smaller audience)
Position as community-first, fair prices, anti-scalper (chosen)
decisions.decision: Position as community-first, fair prices, anti-scalper
decisions.reasoning: Aligns with the actual values of the Pokémon TCG community. Builds long-term trust and repeat buyers. Avoids the race-to-the-bottom problem.
decisions.outcome: Brand tone established as 'fair prices, no nonsense'.

decisions.context: Some Whatnot sellers use wheel spins, lottery formats, and random-draw mechanics to drive engagement.
options_considered: Use gambling mechanics like other sellers (potentially higher short-term engagement)
decisions.reasoning: Gambling mechanics undermine trust and are inconsistent with the fairness-oriented brand. They can also create regulatory risk.
decisions.outcome: Established as a brand red line.

decisions.context: Korean products were initially experimental but have performed better than expected.
options_considered: Promote Korean to staple product line (chosen)
decisions.decision: Promote Korean to staple product line
decisions.reasoning: Korean products have shown consistent performance and good margins. They offer good value to customers.
decisions.outcome: Korean products now considered a useful staple alongside Japanese.

decisions.context: Too many bundle types were slowing streams down and confusing viewers.
options_considered: Continue with many bundle types (more options for buyers)
decisions.reasoning: Stream pacing suffers with too many options. Simple bundles are easier to explain and faster to sell.
decisions.outcome: Simple bundle structures established as the default.
